1.3: 21. (Compare Pr 16:1 , 9 ; Psa 33:10 , 11 ). The failure of man's devices is implied.
1.15:
1. mocker--

Pr 20:1-30 . scorner. Such men are made by wine. made by spicing wine (compare Is 5:11 , 22 ); and it may include wine. or boisterous as a drunkard. literally, "erring," or reeling.

2.4: 11. The conduct of children even is the best test of principle (compare Mt 7:16 ).
2.10:
16. Take his garment--

implies severe exaction, justified by the surety's rashness. by some readings "strangers," but the former here, and in Pr 27:13 , is allowable, and strengthens the sense. The debauchee is less reliable than the merely careless.
2.16:
21. gotten hastily--

contrary to God's providence ( Pr 28:20 ), implying its unjust or easy attainment; hence the man is punished, or spends freely what he got easily (compare Pr 20:17 ).
2.22:
bringeth ... over them--

26. (Compare Pr 20:8 ). The wheel was used for threshing grain. The figure denotes severity (compare Am 1:3 ).

3.11: 5. The contrast is between steady industry and rashness (compare Pr 19:2 ).
3.17: 10. So strongly does he desire to do evil ( Psa 10:3 ; Ec 8:11 ), that he will not even spare his friend if in his way.
3.23: 15. But the just love right and need no bribes. The wicked at last meet destruction, though for a time happy in concealing corruption.
3.29:
spend--

20. The wise, by diligence and care, lay up and increase wealth, while fools literally, "swallow it up," greedily.
3.35:
25. desire--

that is, of ease and idleness brings him to starvation.
3.41: 30-31. Men's best devices and reliances are vain compared with God's, or without His aid ( Pr 19:21 ; Psa 20:7 ; 33:17 ).
3.47:
3. are punished--

that is, for their temerity; for the evil is not necessarily punitive, as the prudent might otherwise be its objects.
3.53:
the rod ... fail--

8. (Compare Pr 11:18 ; Psa 109:16-20 ; Ga 6:7 , 8 ). His power to do evil will be destroyed.
3.59: 13. Frivolous excuses satisfy the indolent man's conscience.
3.65:
fitted--

18. These lessons must be laid up in the mind, and or better, "fixed" in the lips so as to be ever ready.

4.4:
in the gate--

22-23. Here follow ten precepts of two verses each. Though men fail to defend the poor, God will ( Pr 17:5 ; Psa 12:5 ). place of public gathering ( Job 5:4 ; Psa 69:12 ).
4.10: 28. (Compare Pr 23:10 ). Do not entrench on others ( De 19:14 ; 27:17 ).
4.16:
3. are deceitful meat--

though well tasted, injurious.
4.22:
evil eye--

6-8. Beware of deceitful men, whose courtesies even you will repent of having accepted. or purpose ( Pr 22:9 ; De 15:9 ; Mt 6:23 ).

5.5: 13-14. While there is little danger that the use of the "divine ordinance of the rod" will produce bodily harm, there is great hope of spiritual good.
5.11: 17-18. (Compare Margin). The prosperity of the wicked is short.
5.17:
23. Buy--

literally, "get" ( Pr 4:5 ). generally and specially as opposed to errors of all kinds.
